beats.activity: Wake up, strong coffee, check weather and messages at home
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning ritual; Felix wakes naturally on a calm day, eases into the day with coffee and his recent foraging book before deciding how to spend the day.

beats.activity: Walk to neighborhood coffee shop for a pastry and second coffee
beats.reasoning: Sunday ritual in his neighborhood; staying local and hyper-local to savor the morning without committing to a long drive.

beats.activity: Return home, read foraging book on porch, plan the day
beats.reasoning: Felix is in a reflective, low-energy mood from yesterday (40/100); he's content to linger at home with his recent find, letting the sunny weather and calm Sunday vibe guide him.

beats.activity: Commute to Green Lake for a foraging walk and to scout mushroom spots
beats.reasoning: Sunny Sunday is perfect for exploring; Felix heads to Green Lake to combine leisure with his mushroom-foraging hobby, a nostalgic and meditative activity that fits his mood.

beats.activity: Foraging walk around Green Lake, exploring wooded areas and taking notes on future designs
beats.reasoning: A 2+ hour walk lets Felix immerse himself in nature, scout mushroom habitats, and gather visual inspiration for botanical tattoo designs—combines exercise, foraging passion, and creative work.

beats.activity: Lunch at a casual spot near Green Lake
beats.reasoning: After a long walk, Felix grabs lunch nearby; staying in the Green Lake neighborhood keeps him in a meditative, low-key Sunday mood.

beats.activity: Commute to Capitol Hill to browse Elliott Bay Book Company
beats.reasoning: Felix can't resist a bookstore; the sunny afternoon is ideal for a drive across town to Capitol Hill, and he may find more design or foraging references.

beats.activity: Browse Elliott Bay, chat with staff, sketch design ideas
beats.reasoning: Felix lingers in his favorite bookstore, drawing inspiration from design and nature books, a nostalgic Sunday ritual that aligns with his hyper-focused, introspective mood.

beats.activity: Walk around Capitol Hill, grab a coffee and snack at a neighborhood cafe
beats.reasoning: Lazy Sunday afternoon; Felix takes a walk, enjoys the calm weather, and takes time to process the day before heading home.
Drive home as evening approaches; the scenic route through Wallingford feels natural given Felix's nostalgic mood and love of the city's light.

beats.activity: Home evening: read foraging book, sketch new botanical tattoo designs, make dinner, listen to music
beats.reasoning: Felix winds down at home with his books and sketches, reflecting on the day's foraging walk and design inspiration; a quiet, introspective Sunday evening that honors his low energy and love of stillness.
Felix goes to bed satisfied after a restorative Sunday; 7.5 hours of sleep will recharge him for the week ahead.

diary:
Woke up to perfect weather and didn't waste it. Strong coffee at home, then hit Molly Moon's for a pastry—the usual Sunday ritual. Spent the morning on the porch with my foraging book, just soaking it in before heading out.

Green Lake was beautiful. The water was packed with boats and paddleboarders, but the wooded trails stayed quiet. I found some interesting mushroom clusters near the north side and sketched a few ideas right there on the trail. The shapes, the way the gills catch light—there's something about nature that makes better design fuel than anything else. Grabbed lunch at Spud's, nothing fancy but it hit right.

Cap Hill called to me after, so I wandered through Elliott Bay for a couple hours. Talked shop with one of the staff about a new mycology guide they just got in. The afternoon light through the bookstore windows was golden. Coffee at Victrola, then back home to sketch. Made pasta, put on some music, and just... existed. These days feel rare.
thought_process: Today was about feeding the well, I think. I've been running the studio pretty hard lately, so I deliberately avoided even thinking about work. The foraging walk wasn't just exercise—it was research, inspiration, the thing that actually makes my designs better. I was drawn to natural spaces and quiet moments, probably because the city can get loud. The bookstore trip and wandering Cap Hill felt like I was looking for something without knowing what. Maybe just permission to slow down. The weather helped; that kind of perfect Sunday doesn't come often, and I didn't want to waste it indoors. By evening I felt recharged enough to actually sketch, which is when I know my cup is full again.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
